title: Letters to the Editor
summary: | | | GAS-AND-DASH OFFENSES RISE WITH PRICES By Lisa Singhania The Associated Press (Published June 23, 2000) Last summer, each of Bill Douglass' nine convenience stores lost an average of $50 a month to motorists who drove off without paying. This summer, he expects his losses to be at least 10 times higher. "Overall we're averaging $500, but in our largest store it's up to $1,000 per month," said the Sherman, Texas, businessman. "We're taking all kinds of measures, installing video cameras, photographing license plates. This is a very serious problem." With prices at the pump soaring, convenience stores and service stations are seeing more motorists gassing up and driving away without paying. "It's the price of gas and misdirected consumer anger. They may not blame the gas store owner or…

title: Letters to the Editor
summary: | | | ANGLER SHOOTS RUSSIAN RIVER BEAR, ANGERS WITNESSES FEDERAL AUTHORITIES INVESTIGATE SUNDAY INCIDENT By Peter Porco Daily News Reporter (Published August 1, 2000) A young black bear that had become a favorite of fishermen and campers along the Russian River was shot Sunday afternoon by an Anchorage fisherman who told investigators he feared for his daughter's life. The shooting, along a stretch of the river popular with salmon anglers, set off an angry reaction from campers and other fishermen in the area. They yelled at the man and even at Bruce Bigelow, a U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service police officer, Bigelow said. Bigelow, one of the first authorities on the scene about 2:30 p.m., shot at the head of the motionless animal to be certain it was dead, he said. In the confusion, some ca…
